resolved.rca_reason_detail:
Reason for Churn Signal

The client raised a ticket 11 days ago regarding vehicle images reverting to older versions. From the initial investigation, it appeared to be a loop issue that required urgent attention and coordination between teams.

Throughout this period, I repeatedly escalated the issue in meetings and through support channels, highlighting both the urgency and the potential business impact. Despite multiple follow-ups, the issue remained unresolved and the ticket exceeded the expected SLA.

After several unsuccessful attempts to reach a resolution, I proactively involved both Dealerslink and Spyne support to facilitate joint troubleshooting and identify the root cause. However, the Dealerslink coordination ticket was merged into an existing ticket before any response or engagement with Dealerslink had taken place, which unfortunately delayed progress further.

Relevant tickets:

Ticket #16474: https://spyne.freshdesk.com/a/tickets/16474

Ticket #16756: https://spyne.freshdesk.com/a/tickets/16756 (merged before a response was provided to Dealerslink)

Despite additional escalations after the ticket merge, the issue remained unaddressed for several more days.

Current Status / Resolution Plan

Support has now engaged with Dealerslink and confirmed the issue stems from a loop caused by configuration settings on the Dealerslink side. We are working to obtain written confirmation from Dealerslink that this is not a Spyne-specific issue but a broader integration/configuration issue.

In parallel, I have aligned with Vishnu and Vanshit on the recovery plan. Vishnu has an established relationship with Paul Grace (GM at one of the stores) and has previously met him in person. Together, we are executing an account recovery and churn mitigation plan while ensuring complete resolution of the underlying issue.

Blockers

The primary blocker throughout this process has been the delay in support engagement and resolution despite repeated escalations and the time-sensitive nature of the issue.

Help Needed

I need the ticket to be prioritized and fully resolved so that I can move forward with the account recovery plan. Once the issue is closed and the client receives a complete resolution, I am confident we can effectively execute the churn mitigation strategy and work toward recovering the account.

Currently, I am working closely with Vanshit, Vishnu, and the support team to bring the account back on track.

False Churn Alert Summary – World Car (Brent Smithson)

A churn concern was triggered after a discussion regarding the deployment of an AI Receptionist/Operator agent, where the customer was informed that a standalone operator agent may involve additional commercial considerations not covered under the current agreement.

During the call, Brent expressed frustration, primarily due to the fact that multiple prior discussions around the operator use case had not included any mention of incremental costs. He highlighted that World Car has been a strong advocate for Spyne, actively participating in beta testing, providing product feedback, supporting customer references, and helping shape future AI use cases such as the Retention Agent.

However, throughout the conversation, Brent repeatedly reaffirmed his commitment to the partnership and made it clear that his concern was around expectation setting and contract clarity, not dissatisfaction with Spyne or intent to discontinue the relationship. He specifically stated that he remains fully invested in Spyne’s success, is willing to continue supporting product innovation, and wants to be an early adopter of future solutions including the Retention Agent.

The Spyne team clarified that:
• The intent was never to introduce an unexpected charge but to align with internal finance requirements.
• An alternative solution using existing Sales/Service agents could be deployed immediately to achieve the desired business outcome.
• The team would continue working internally to enable the standalone operator use case without impacting the customer experience.

Brent accepted the proposed workaround and agreed that immediate deployment through existing agents would satisfy the current requirement while Spyne evaluates the standalone implementation. The call concluded positively, with both parties aligned on next steps and reaffirming the strategic nature of the partnership.

Outcome: No churn risk identified. Customer remains highly engaged, committed to the partnership, and actively discussing future expansion opportunities including Retention AI and large scale outbound engagement programs. This should be categorized as a false churn alert driven by a commercial misunderstanding rather than a true retention risk.
